name: Tom
observedShift: Tom moved teams recently and delivery is steady, but Principal-level impact is not visible yet. Previous feedback said he needs early expectation alignment, and he still asks very few questions. I want to test whether he understands what Principal scope means on this team or if he is still operating on old assumptions. I also need to understand whether cross-team trust and judgment calls are developing or whether he is staying in safe execution mode.

focusPoints.label: Principal-level impact and visible leverage
focusPoints.reason: Whether his Principal scope is showing up in outcomes yet, or whether delivery is steady but still mostly execution. Previous feedback suggests the gap could be expectation alignment and the visibility of leverage — worth clarifying what he thinks counts as Principal impact on this team.

focusPoints.label: What Principal scope means on this team
focusPoints.reason: What 'good' looks like for a Principal on this team is the anchor for performance conversations — it prevents drifting on old assumptions after a team move and makes feedback actionable.

focusPoints.label: Cross-team trust and hard-news communication
focusPoints.reason: Whether he’s building trust beyond his immediate team by setting expectations early, sharing context, and escalating hard news appropriately — or staying in safe execution mode.

focusPoints.label: Judgment calls: when to push vs escalate
focusPoints.reason: How his tradeoff calls are maturing is a core Principal performance signal: does he calibrate risks, decide under ambiguity, and know when to handle vs bring you in.

focusPoints.label: Early expectation alignment and question-asking
focusPoints.reason: Whether he’s asking the right questions early enough now, or whether he’s still operating with too many assumptions. The notes say he asks very few questions — worth testing if that’s confidence, preference, or a gap in alignment.

issues: listenFor may lack observable behavioural cue: "whether if they describe Principal scope, they talk about le…"
